tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows markedly elevated serum creatinine and significant proteinuria, suggesting impaired renal function, possibly nephrotic syndrome or acute/chronic kidney disease. Urine findings (cloudy, brown, low specific gravity, glucosuria, ketonuria, proteinuria) further support renal dysfunction and possible metabolic derangement. No evidence of urinary tract infection. Recommend urgent nephrology evaluation and further assessment for underlying causes, including diabetes and glomerular disease.

display.value: Proteinuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
Nonproliferative retinopathy due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Retinopathy due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
